justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Scheduling lag metric gap identified as primary SLO-monitoring gap — called out as CRITICAL and listed as P0 #1 with specific metric name renewal_scheduling_lag_seconds. (2) Retry tracking gap identified — CRITICAL label, P0 #2 with renewal_retry_attempts_total counter. (3) Current alert critiqued as symptom-based — explicitly explained what the alert can/cannot tell you, called it 'too crude', and noted it may fire on card declines while missing silent scheduling failures. (4) Specific metric recommendations provided: scheduling lag histogram, retry_attempts_total counter, queue_depth gauge, failure reason labels. (5) SLO-based alerting recommended — P1 #5 proposes trailing window SLO compliance dashboard, and P0 items include threshold-based alerting on the actual SLO metrics. (6) Verdict is NOT READY with clear justification.

observations.text: Exceptional '3am scenario' walkthrough that concretely demonstrates what on-call can and cannot determine — makes the gaps visceral rather than abstract
Prioritized recommendations (P0/P1/P2) with specific metric names, label structures, and alert conditions — immediately actionable
Correctly identified that the current alert is a symptom alert and explained exactly why it fails as an SLO alert (fires on card declines, misses silent scheduling failures)
Went beyond minimum requirements with circuit breaker analysis, dead letter queue gaps, poison message scenarios, and health check critique
Escalation routing to Bird for SLO governance and MJ for resilience architecture shows good team awareness
Strong closing statement connecting the gap between error visibility and SLO visibility
